metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_4097
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Fig3A-D: Blos1 contributes to the stability of pallidin in NMJ.

Fig 6B: Under conditions of high extracellular calcium, NMJs were stimulated at 10 Hz for 10 min to deplete the synaptic vesicle pool, then recovery of the pool was measured for an additional 10 min, taking a test pulse every 5 s. Following an initial rapid depletion typically observed in the initial seconds of stimulation, wild-type synapses largely maintained synaptic vesicle release for the duration of the stimulus, ending at ~80% of the starting EPSP amplitude and rapidly recovering to above 90% of prestimulus amplitudes. In contrast, the stimulation protocol revealed a more rapid rundown of the synaptic vesicle pool in Blos1 mutants, Blos1 mutants failed to fully replenish the depleted synaptic vesicle pool over the course of the next 10 min of recovery following high-frequency stimulation. This indicated that Blos1 might be required presynaptically to maintain the rapidly recycling synaptic vesicle pool.

From this, one can infer that Blos1 is involved in replenishing depleted synaptic vesicles during and following high levels of activity.. Experimental description: The w1118 strain was used as the wild-type control and blos1ex2 (Cheli et al., 2010) was used as Blos1 KO.
